title: oxytropis lambertii subsp. bigelovii
intro: Oxytropis lambertii subsp. bigelovii is a rare, perennial legume plant native to North America, commonly known as Bigelow's locoweed. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's roots and leaves contain isoflavones and other compounds with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are believed to provide several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and supporting cardiovascular health. Oxytropis lambertii subsp. bigelovii can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
